page: Amazon Web Services m6i.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, configured with 8 dedicated virtual CPUs derived from 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It lacks local NVMe storage and does not include any GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the m6i.2xlarge in the average performance tier for most standard workloads. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, consistently fall within the middle 50% of tested systems. Memory read-only bandwidth reaches 413.91 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 58.41 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. The instance demonstrates average throughput in database operations, Redis SET operations at 7.98 million operations per second, and static web serving at 11.87 GB/sec. However, it exhibits performance limitations in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-sized 7B LLM inference tasks, where it scores in the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6i.2xlarge offers a balanced resource density profile, providing standard compute and memory allocations without the added expense of specialized hardware like local SSDs or GPUs. This makes it a practical option for general-purpose workloads that do not require high-speed local storage. Recommended workloads include static web serving, standard database operations, software compilation, and lightweight text or image processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m6i.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps but lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute, memory, and database operations, including a Redis SET throughput of 7.98 million operations per second. However, performance drops for complex tasks, with weak multi-threaded decompression and poor 7B LLM inference speeds. This instance is designed for balanced workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and standard database hosting where local NVMe storage is not required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8375C processor, delivering balanced performance for standard workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 8 vCPUs, 32.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, built on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute and memory profile designed for standard web serving, database operations, and general-purpose application hosting.

Baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Average performance in standard database operations and static web serving
